title: Project Employees III
description: Find employees with the most experience for each project. Include ties.
constraints: (project_id, employee_id) is the primary key of Project
employee_id is the primary key of Employee
Multiple employees can share the maximum experience on a project — all must be returned
key_concept: Filter with (project_id, experience_years) IN (SELECT project_id, MAX(experience_years) ...).

hints.L1: For each project, find the maximum experience. Return all employees with that max.
hints.L2: (p.project_id, e.experience_years) IN (SELECT project_id, MAX(exp) from joined table GROUP BY project_id)
hints.L3: SELECT p.project_id, p.employee_id FROM Project p JOIN Employee e ON p.employee_id = e.employee_id WHERE (p.project_id, e.experience_years) IN (SELECT p2.project_id, MAX(e2.experience_years) FROM Project p2 JOIN Employee e2 ON p2.employee_id = e2.employee_id GROUP BY p2.project_id)
